reviews.review_msg: He doesn't care at all about students and gives out of syllabus exams . And nothing came from quizzes or study plan from mypearson in all midterms and final exams.

Took Econ 103 & 105 with this Prof. The course is generally pretty easy. Contrary to the comments below. I find this this prof to be a good person. He listens to students and trys his best to help the students understand.
Quite a good teacher. Mid-terms and exams are based mostly on lecture notes.

This professor is useless! he doesnt know how to teach nor does he know how to communicate effectively with his students! He has a VERY bad attitude and i want to warn this guy that if he keeps up with this kind of attitude towards his students, he WILL lose his job, its a matter of time! Exams were brutal, save ur money AND your GPA!

His class is not all that hard, however, this guy has a really bad attitude... very arrogant, not very helpful... if you go to get help, he will think you are stupid and wont help much... his notes are also very hard to read... very very very mean prof... T^T take class at your own risk !!

Wow, this class was almost too easy. His assignments are east marks and the midterm isn't that hard either. This guy's only downfall is that he may do TOO MUCH review. He spends half an hour each lecture going over the stuff he taught last lecture. If you don't do well in this course it's your fault and you shouldn't even be in University!!
Nobody in class laughs at his jokes but he's a pretty funny guy. He talks very loud, it is amusing but he is quite clear in his lectures. Heavy textbook use. Difficult.

Had lectures online but attending is quite useful because he writes the notes on the projector. Lecture notes and text go almost hand in hand. It's frustrating that he doesn't curve even when the second midterm average was extremely low. Fairly good at explaining economic concepts.

In the beginning of the semester, I thought this guy was really nice and the class was easy.But when I actually went to get one-on-one help, he was an a$$!He's totally biased to his students.If he likes u then he's really nice to u but if he doesn't,then you won't be getting any help for the rest of the semester! BIASED!!

He's great prof! Very clear, explains everything important from the readings. Definitely not a boring class he always kept me interested and focused. His notes were very helpful. The Qs from the midterm were EXACTLY the same as the notes and Qs in tutorial. Attend class...do the readings and participate in tutorial and it's all that it takes!!!
not helpful at all and very sarcastic to his students with a bad attitude. marks hard and thinks his jokes are funny when they arnt. BAD

He is an okay prof. His exams are mainly based on math problems done in tutorials, so as long as you practice with them you are fine. But he gave one question on our mt that he hadn't gone over in lecture. and that question is 10% of the mt. And after the TA went over the answer, i had no idea what she was talking about

He has his office hour everyday, but I guess it is his temper that keeps students away from asking him (at least, I cant stand his temper myself). I want him to know that asking questions does not mean we want trouble, nor we are not hard working. If we can understand everything by just reading, what do we need u for? In short, u r not the worst.
A bit biased, midterm average was very low 57% but willing to weigh final more. Final was very fair, gave study guide. Overall, course is interesting.
exam was too long and tedious with numerous multiple choice questions. he tries to make lectures interesting but unsucessful. overall, this is not a good course with heavy workload.

very very bad writing...but he posts good online notes anyways.  overall a pretty easy course, assignments were super easy, quizzes were tricky, the midterm/final were fair.  the m/c on the exams were actually harder than the long questions...
Super bad prof.notes are not clear.You need to study by yourself. Grading are hard. super mean prof. The worse prof I have in SFU. Even worse than Jiu..
Bad.
he's a good prof.. very interesting and knows his stuff well.. study guide prove to be very useful and his midterms are forgiveable since his finals are much harder than the midterm
Very clear prof. Easy to do well as long as you put in the effort. The textbook was not needed, his online notes and notes from class are more then enough.
Lectures were kinda boring. He tried to be funny and failed almost every single time. The course itself is not hard, just study the study guide and online notes you will be fine

Very clear prof. All quizes and exams are questions straight from the study guide so use the study guide often! Goes through the book in lectures so I'd just listen to his lecture notes online while going through the book if you miss a class. Relatively easy if you study. Recommended!

Ok. Listen up! This guy is the best! Since 98% of econ profs are sadistically hard, you've hit the jackpot if u end up with this guy for econ 105! mark breakdown is easy and his only midterm is very easy. For econ 103 go with Phil Curry, hes super easy too. As for econ profs, stick with this guy or phil curry for any econ course. You'll thank me!
You need the Study guide as most of the tests are questions directly from it

Great prof!! you dont really need to read the text book b/c his lectures are very clear and has great notes. Mediocre jokes, highly recommend taking his class, his exams are fairly easy, all from the study guide and lectures
interesting + fun
His excellent notes, clear lectures and easy exams make it worth listening to his terrible jokes :) highly recommend taking this class with him.
good prof!!! very helpful if you need his help!!! go to his lectures!! fun and helpful!!! take his course if you see him!!!

umm...his exams are not too hard, but you do really have to read the questions very carefully, because you might even get confused with a very simple question. The notes are a bit too brief. In the exam, there is always mc questions with true, false, and uncertain. It takes you a lot of time to think in that part...sometimes.

Environmental Econ - yes he over do it when explaining concepts with his jokes and emphasis. Assignments are not too difficult but needs you to fully understand the material to do it. Sometimes overly arrogant/bias when replying to questions. I was failing going into the final. But got B- at the end(final). But I wouldn't take a course w/ him again

His characters swings between over-the-top to bumbling idiot. Doesn't really know what he is teaching; lots of forced dramatic emphasis and desperately unbearable jokes. Questions asked in assignments, quizzes, even the midterm are unfair -- requires unnecessary and unasked for calculations to "fully" answer simpler questions.

had his first lecture yesterday. i found him pretty exaggerating, and biased. Well, he ended up embarassing himself when a girl ask him to explain why&how human breathing is polluting air. he pushes too hard on the students, and tries to hard to get ppl to agree with him. i m disappointed. he totally needs to work on lecturing skills.

Amazing voice I have to say and he makes calculus fun.He likes logics, ( same as PK ) so you'd better know what you are writing on exams. Overall, he is a fair prof and provides extra helps if you needed.
Helpful. Clear. Interesting

a helpful and responsible professor, this course covers so much stuff but it's not that hard actually. However, assignments and exams are very long, you have to spend a lot of time and effort to get a good grade

this prof is very helpful, very clear, exams are  fair, not very hard.  But this is not an easy course, i spent so much time on it.  You'll never understand everything in the course.  After all, he is good
Very very nice Prof.  Strongly recommend.  Always wearing tie @ class which is the only prof does that i've ever seen. :) very helpful
